name: Correction Review — Practising Your Own Past Mistakes
_note: Used by the dynamic 'Practice My Mistakes' session. The checklist is EMPTY here and is filled at runtime by review_builder (one item per target correction). Standard 4 metric keys re-pitched: medical_accuracy = whether the improved phrases were produced, clinical_reasoning slot = retention (avoiding the old mistakes).

anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear

anchors.9-10: Produced the improved version of every target item naturally
anchors.5-6: Produced about half of the target items
anchors.1-2: Did not produce the improved forms

anchors.9-10: Never fell back into the old error patterns
Smooth, confident delivery of the improved phrasing
anchors.5-6: Noticeable hesitation on the new phrasing
anchors.3-4: Frequent stops when reaching for the improved form

anchors.9-10: Exceptional empathy and professionalism; creates psychological safety
anchors.7-8: Good empathy; acknowledges emotion; polite and professional
anchors.5-6: Adequate but mechanical empathy; some emotional cues missed
anchors.3-4: Little empathic response; information delivery prioritized
anchors.1-2: No empathic response; dismissive or unprofessional
